channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Vehicular action game Truck-kun is Supporting Me From Another World?! announced for Xbox Series, PC
item.content:encoded:
Publisher Frosty Pop and developer Strange Scaffold have announced Truck-kun is Supporting Me From Another World?!, a vehicular action game where you play as a truck that “isekais” people into another world. It will launch for Xbox Series and PC via Steam in 2026.

Here is an overview of the game, via Strange Scaffold:

About

Truck-kun is Supporting Me From Another World?! is a vehicular action game where you play as a truck that isekais people into another world.

This anime-inspired title is about smashing through objects at high speed to power up the corporate ladder climber you accidentally isekai’d into a dangerous medieval fantasy world. Inspired by the anime and manga subgenre of Isekai, in which characters find themselves in parallel universes, Truck-kun is Supporting Me From Another World?! revels in absurdism while simultaneously grounding itself with sharp satire that reflects real life.

Players start their journey with Carissa Ward, who is about to start a new job as VP of marketing in the sunny coastal town of Wenvale. That plan is cut short when she’s struck by a delivery truck and awakens in the fantasy world of Talinfold. To get home, she must unlock the Galactic Gate, defeat the Skeleton King, and claim his Scroll of Resurrection—with your help.

Good luck!!!

Key Features

  • Dodge cops, make deliveries, and smash through objects in the chaotic small town of Wenvale, USA!
  • Complete objectives across two worlds to power up a Galactic Gate with stars and unlock a subversive, heartwarming story rooted in our love for anime.
  • Run over pedestrians to turn them into monsters Carissa can slay for stars and XP. It’s fine! Don’t worry! Stop thinking about it!!
  • Energetic, ska-inspired soundtrack by award-winning composer David Mason (DREDGE, Creepy Redneck Dinosaur Mansion 3).
  • A giant, screaming man whose meat you can legally drive through.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/vehicular-action-game-truck-kun-is-supporting-me-from-another-world-announced-for-xbox-series-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Famitsu Sales: 4/20/26 – 4/26/26
item.content:encoded:
• PS5
  • Switch
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox Series

Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream remains on top.

Famitsu has published its estimated physical game software data for Japan for the week of April 20, 2026 to April 26, 2026.

Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream for Switch remained the highest selling game this week, moving another 178,533 retail copies.

The Switch 2 version of PRAGMATA also launched this week following the PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC launch the previous week, moving 14,453 units. The PlayStation 5 version also sold an additional 12,786 units.

On the hardware side, Switch 2 sold 45,825 units, the Switch family sold 27,139 units, the PlayStation 5 family sold 12,973 units, and the Xbox Series family sold 247 units.

Get the full sales charts below.

Software Sales (followed by lifetime sales)

  1. [NSW] Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream (Nintendo, 04/16/26) – 178,533 (743,938)
  2. [SW2] Pokemon Pokopia (The Pokemon Company, 03/05/26) – 17,039 (927,044)
  3. [SW2] PRAGMATA (Capcom, 04/24/26) – 14,453 (New)
  4. [PS5] PRAGMATA (Capcom, 04/17/26) – 12,786 (49,256)
  5. [NSW] Matsurika no Kei -kEi- Tenmei kashokuden (Idea Factory, 04/23/26) – 7,654 (New)
  6. [SW2] Mario Kart World (Nintendo, 06/05/25) – 5,537 (2,906,379)
  7. [SW2] Animal Crossing: New Horizons – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Nintendo, 01/15/26) – 3,695 (108,612)
  8. [NSW] Minecraft (Microsoft, 06/21/18) – 3,378 (4,193,529)
  9. [NSW] Momotaro Dentetsu 2: Anata no Machi mo Kitto Aru – Higashi Nihon Hen + Nishi Nihon Hen (Konami, 11/13/25) – 3,201 (343,741)
  10. [NSW] Elminage ORIGINAL: Priestess of Darkness and The Ring of the Gods (Mebius, 04/23/26) – 2,882 (New)

Rankings 11 to 30 will be announced on May 1.

  1. [NSW] Kingdom’s Return: Time-Eating Fruit and the Ancient Monster (Inti Creates, 04/23/26) – (New)
  2. [NSW] Animal Crossing: New Horizons (Nintendo, 03/20/20) –
  3. [NSW] Doraemon Dorayaki Shop Story (Kairosoft, 04/16/26) –
  4. [NSW] Nintendo Switch Sports (NIntendo, 04/29/22) –
  5. [NSW] Pokemon FireRed Version / LeafGreen Version (Download Card) (The Pokemon Company, 02/28/26) –
  6. [SW2] Xenoblade Chronicles X: Definitive Edition – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Nintendo, 04/16/26) –
  7. [NSW] Super Smash Bros. Ultimate (Nintendo, 12/07/18) –
  8. [PS5] Crimson Desert (Pearl Abyss, 03/19/26) –
  9. [SW2] Super Mario Bros. Wonder – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Meetup in Bellabel Park (Nintendo, 03/26/26) –
  10. [NSW] Mario Kart 8 Deluxe (Nintendo, 04/28/17) –
  11. [SW2] Pokemon Legends: Z-A – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (The Pokemon Company, 10/16/25) –
  12. [SW2] Resident Evil Requiem (Capcom, 02/27/26) –
  13. [NSW] Pokemon Legends: Z-A (The Pokemon Company, 10/16/25) –
  14. [SW2] Mario Tennis Fever (Nintendo, 02/12/26) –
  15. [SW2] Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection (Capcom, 03/13/26) –
  16. [NSW] Super Mario Galaxy + Super Mario Galaxy 2 (Nintendo, 10/02/25) –
  17. [SW2] Super Mario Party Jamboree – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Jamboree TV (Nintendo, 07/24/25) –
  18. [NSW] Super Mario Party Jamboree (Nintendo, 10/17/24) –
  19. [SW2] Momotaro Dentetsu 2: Anata no Machi mo Kitto Aru – Higashi Nihon Hen + Nishi Nihon Hen – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Konami, 11/13/25) –
  20. [PS5] Resident Evil Requiem (Capcom, 02/27/26) –
  21. [NSW] Dragon Quest VII Reimagined (Square Enix, 02/05/26) –
  22. [NSW] Splatoon 3 (Nintendo, 09/09/22) –
  23. [NSW] Clubhouse Games: 51 Worldwide Classics (Nintendo, 06/05/20) –
  24. [SW2] Kirby Air Riders (Nintendo, 11/20/25) –

Hardware Sales (followed by lifetime sales)

  1. Switch 2 – 45,825 (5,153,222)
  2. Switch Lite – 11,263 (6,942,368)
  3. Switch OLED Model – 10,796 (9,554,639)
  4. PlayStation 5 Digital Edition – 6,361 (1,266,803)
  5. Switch – 5,080 (20,284,100)
  6. PlayStation 5 Pro – 4,330 (349,972)
  7. PlayStation 5 – 2,282 (5,916,138)
  8. Xbox Series X Digital Edition – 127 (30,138)
  9. Xbox Series S – 82 (341,861)
  10. Xbox Series X – 37 (326,322)


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/famitsu-sales-4-20-26-4-26-26
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Wars: Galactic Racer launches October 6
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Available in standard, Deluxe, and Collector's editions.

Star Wars: Galactic Racer will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store on October 8, publisher Secret Mode and developer Fuse Games announced.

The game will be available in the following editions:

  • Standard Edition ($59.99 / £49.99 / €59.99)
    • A copy of the game
  • Deluxe Edition ($79.99 / £64.99 / €79.99)
    • A copy of the game
    • Steel case with sleeve (physical edition only)
    • Digital art book – Contains never-before-seen artwork of the characters, locations, and vehicles
    • Exclusive repulsorcrafts (x3)
      • Kor Sarun: Darc X landspeeder
      • Kor Sarun: Ciza T speeder bike,
      • Kor Sarun: Rak S skim speeder
    • Unique arcade events (x3)
    • Deluxe Livery Pack – Unique designs for all of your vehicles
    • Deluxe Player Banner Pack – New Insignia and Background for you to show your style in multiplayer modes
  • Collector’s Edition ($159.99 / £139.99 / €159.9)
    • Kor Sarun: Darc X landspeeder physical model
    • Galactic League champion Kestar Bool physical banner
    • Pilot patches (x2)
    • Physical art book
    • Steel case with custom slip cover
    • Deluxe Edition upgrade – Includes all the digital items from the Deluxe Edition

Pre-orders for all editions include a free bonus livery which can be used with landspeeders, speeder bikes, or skim speeders, with a unique color for the livery depending on the pre-order platform. All pre-orders also include a free Player Banner background for use in multiplayer modes.

  • ❮
  • ❯

Here is an overview of the game, via Secret Mode:

About

Star Wars: Galactic Racer is a runs-based, high-stakes reinvention of racing born in the lawless Outer Rim of the Star Wars galaxy.

The Empire’s grip on the galaxy has finally broken, and with its fall comes a new obsession: speed. As the New Republic struggles to rebuild, gambling, entertainment, and glory fuel a booming underground.

Out of this chaos, The Galactic League is born: an unsanctioned racing circuit where syndicates sponsor pilots, fortunes are won and lost in seconds, and only the bold survive.

No Force. No prophecy. Just skill, strategy, and the will to rise.

Key Features

  • Compete in High-stakes Races Where Every Run is Unique – Slam, shunt, and takedown rivals across varied circuits where no two runs play the same. The greater the risk, the greater the reward.
  • Survive a Dangerous Underground Sport – Join The Galactic League – an unsanctioned racing circuit born in the lawless Outer Rim. Become Shade, a lone racer chasing revenge and glory.
  • Build and Customize Your Craft  – Pilot different classes of repulsorcraft each with distinct physics and playstyles. Build a ride that showcases your style and gives you a competitive edge.
  • Battle Rivals in Multiple Game Modes – Ride solo through a story-driven campaign of shifting alliances or brave strategic player-versus-player grudge matches where reputation rides with you.
  • Climb the Ranks and Earn Rewards – Prove yourself through intense rivalries and escalating challenges. Every choice shapes your prospects as you build your status in The Galactic League.

Watch a new trailer below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/star-wars-galactic-racer-launches-october-6
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nacon Connect 2026 set for May 7
item.content:encoded:
Updates, announcements, and surprises abound.

Following a delay from its previously planned March 4 air date, Nacon Connect 2026 will now air on May 7 at 11:00 a.m. PT / 2:00 p.m. ET, Nacon announced.

The broadcast will feature “exciting updates on our biggest games, brand-new announcements, and exclusive surprises,” including The Mound, Edge of Memories, Endurance Motorsport Series, and Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish.

Watch a teaser trailer below.

Teaser Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/nacon-connect-2026-set-for-may-7
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dark fantasy roguelike deckbuilder Rogue Reigns announced for PS5, Xbox Series, Switch, PC, iOS, and Android
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Xbox Series

Due out in September.

Brazilian developer Venn Studios has announced Rogue Reigns, a dark fantasy roguelike deckbuilder. It will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch, PC via Steam, iOS, and Android in September. Demos will be playable at Gamescom LATAM 2026 from April 30 to May 3, and Gamescom 2026 from August 26 to 30. It will also be featured as part of the Steam Deckbuilders Fest from May 4 to 11.

“In most deckbuilders, you control one character and build one deck,” Venn Studios CEO Hugo Neri said in a press release. “In Rogue Reigns, three characters fight together—and the way they interact is the game. Your party composition is your build.”

Here is an overview of the game, via Venn Studios:

About

Rogue Reigns is a dark fantasy roguelike deckbuilder featuring hand-drawn characters set against procedurally generated 3D environments—a rarity within the deckbuilding genre—and an innovative party-based combat system.

Developed using Venn Studios’ proprietary Wildcard engine, Rogue Reigns represents a major technical and creative leap for the genre, blending hand-crafted character art and stylized 3D environments with the strategic depth of roguelike deckbuilding.

A New Evolution of Deckbuilding: “Your Party Is Your Build”

At its core, Rogue Reigns reimagines traditional deckbuilding by shifting the focus from a single character to a party of three heroes, each with their own deck, abilities, and turn order. The interplay between characters is where the game truly comes alive.

After every encounter, the party faces a choice: rewards are limited. One hero gets stronger. The others don’t. Over the course of a run, these small decisions accumulate—the hero you’ve been feeding grows powerful while the ones you’ve neglected fall behind. When the stalker catches up, someone falls.

Players will assemble a team from a roster of unique classes—including Warrior, Wizard, Rogue, Paladin, and Warlock—with each combination unlocking new synergies, strategies, and emergent gameplay possibilities. At launch, players can experiment with 10 distinct party compositions, ensuring no two runs feel the same.

A Dark Fantasy World Without Death

Set in fractured kingdoms where death itself has been abolished by a misused divine artifact, Rogue Reigns invites players into a decaying world filled with tragic rulers, broken societies, and slow-burning collapse.

This haunting setting is brought to life through a distinctive hand-crafted 3D art direction, combining bold, expressive character design with muted palettes and striking color accents—delivering a visual identity rarely seen in roguelike deckbuilders.

Built with the Community, for the Community

Rogue Reigns is the first title from Venn Studios, a Sao Paulo-based indie team that has spent years deeply embedded in the deckbuilding community.

Through their YouTube channel—now surpassing 108,000 subscribers—the team has built a reputation as passionate experts in the genre, creating content that explores mechanics, design philosophy, and emerging trends in roguelike deckbuilders.

This close relationship with players has directly shaped the game’s development. Rogue Reigns has been extensively refined through numerous playtests involving dedicated deckbuilder fans across the community, both at live events and through ongoing feedback loops. These sessions have played a key role in evolving the game’s core systems—particularly its party-based mechanics.

Venn Studios plans to continue expanding these playtests leading up to launch, opening them up to a broader audience and ensuring the community remains an active part of the development process.

Key Features

  • Party-Based Deckbuilding – Control three heroes simultaneously, each with their own deck and role.
  • Deep Synergy System – Character interactions create layered, emergent strategies.
  • Trust and Sacrifice – Rewards are scarce. Not every hero benefits equally. Favor one, and the others fall behind.
  • Hand-Drawn Characters, Procedural Worlds – Expressive character art meets generated 3D environments that react to combat.
  • The Stalker – Something hunts your party across the map. The world reacts before you see it.
  • Over 10 Party Compositions at Launch – High replayability with diverse builds and playstyles.
  • Proprietary Wildcard Engine – Built from the ground up for systemic gameplay and visual fidelity.
  • Striking 3D Visual Style – A rare aesthetic in the genre, blending stylization with depth.
  • Community-Driven Development – Shaped by player feedback across global playtests and events.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/dark-fantasy-roguelike-deckbuilder-rogue-reigns-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-switch-pc-ios-and-android
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PopoloCrois creator Yosuke Tamori dies at age 74
item.content:encoded:
Esteemed creator passed away on April 10.

Yosuke Tamori, the manga artist and novelist who created PopoloCrois, passed away at the age 74 on April 10, according to an announcement on the Mangaka Association website.

Tamori first published the PopoloCrois manga in the magazine Dakkusu in 1978. In 1981, he began serialization of the manga series in Asashi Shougakusei Shimbun. In 1996, Sony Computer Entertainment (now Sony Interactive Entertainment) released a video game version of PopoloCrois for the original PlayStation, which spawned multiple sequels. It also received two anime adaptations in 1998 and 2003. Tamori also published various other manga and picture books in his career.

Thanks, Famitsu.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/popolocrois-creator-yosuke-tamori-dies-at-age-74
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
